openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6.output: {"A":"Answer A provides a fully functional joke generator with excellent visual design including animations, confetti effects, 3D card tilt, and a counter. It correctly implements random joke selection on button click, follows all instructions, and includes 4 jokes. It is slightly over-engineered but technically correct and complete.","B":"Answer B provides a clean, functional joke generator with a two-step reveal mechanic (setup first, then punchline on second button click). It follows the core instruction of showing a random joke on button click, includes 4 jokes, has a nice dark theme, and adds a useful extra feature. It is clear, well-explained, and appropriately concise.","winner":"B","reason":"Both answers are correct and functional. Answer B more closely follows the prompt's simplicity requirement ('simple website') and adds a thoughtful two-step punchline reveal that enhances the joke experience without over-engineering. Answer A, while impressive, introduces heavy dependencies (Tailwind CDN), complex confetti animations, 3D tilt effects, and icon toggling that go well beyond 'simple.' Answer B is cleaner, self-contained, easier to understand, and better matches the stated requirement of a simple website where clicking a button shows a joke."}

messages.content: Create a simple website using HTML. When the user clicks a button, one of four jokes should be displayed at random.
